text: Being here, surrounded by the abstraction of a modern exhibition, I sense both stimulation and distance. The clean forms and technological displays evoke curiosity but also make me ponder the sterile, almost clinical side of innovation. I am aware of my own smallness within these vast, open spaces. The silence invites self-reflection, and I find myself wondering about the intersection of human intellect and the cold logic of machines. Is there comfort or discomfort in such settings? I feel intrigued, yet slightly unsettled, reminded of how easily one's presence can vanish among ideas much larger than the self.
